CHINTADA ANAND                PETITIONER(S)
VERSUS 
STATE OF ANDHRA PRADESH & ORS.                RESPONDENT(S)
O R D E R
As per Office Report dated 26.5.2026, the Review Petition is
defective.  It further states that despite the defects being notified,
the petitioner has not cured the defects. 
In the interest of justice, the defects raised by the Registry are
waived.
Application  seeking  oral  hearing  of  the  Review  Petition  is
rejected.
We have gone through the Review Petition and perused the
judgment  dated  24.03.2026  which  has  been  sought  to  be
reviewed.  There is no error apparent on the record.  
The Review Petition is, accordingly, dismissed.
........……………………………….J.
                  [PRASHANT KUMAR MISHRA]
………………………….J.
                                                   [MANMOHAN]
NEW DELHI;
JULY 15, 2026.

VISHNU H                                           Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF KERALA                                    Respondent(s)
IA No. 114694/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
IA No. 114696/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 15-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PRASHANT KUMAR MISHRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SHREE CHANDRASHEKHAR
For Petitioner(s)  Ms. Sweena Madhavan Nair, AOR
                   Ms. Yogita, Adv.
                   Ms. Siny Sara Varghese, Adv.
                   Mr. P Anil Kumar, Adv.
                   Ms. Anjana, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s)  Mr. Harshad V. Hameed, AOR
    Mr. Aljo K. Joseph, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Vide order dated 16.04.2026, this Court has allowed
interim anticipatory bail to the petitioner by making the
following observations:-
"1. Petitioner is the driver of the vehicle in
which, accused Nos. 1 and 2, who happen to be
the husband and mother-in-law of the de-facto
complainant’s  daughter,  have  allegedly
committed house trespass, criminal intimidation

--- Page 759 ---
and assaulted the de-facto complainant causing
undisplaced fracture of the 6th rib. 
2. Material on record shows that accused Nos.1
and 2 have already been released on regular
bail and the present petitioner is not related
to the family."
2. Upon hearing learned counsel for the parties, we are
inclined  to  make  absolute  the  interim  order  dated
16.04.2026.
3. Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition is disposed of,
directing petitioner’s release on anticipatory bail in
connection with FIR No. 257 of 2026 registered at Police
Station Adoor, Pathanamthitta District, Kerala for offences
punishable under  Sections 333, 296(b), 126(2), 115(2),
118(2), 351(2) and 74 read with Section 3(5) of Bharatiya
Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 on such terms and conditions as may be
imposed by the Trial Court, subject to the condition that
the petitioner shall continue to co-operate with the trial/
investigation. 
4.  Pending  applications(s),  if  any,  shall  also  stand
disposed of.
(MINI)                                 (AKSHAY KUMAR BHORIA)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Shri Vikas Singh, learned senior counsel submitted  
that the petitioners do not wish to press the adoption which 
was    under    the    provisions  of  Hindu  Adoption and 
Maintenance Act, 1956 (for short, `HAMA’) in view of the fact 
Contd..

--- Page 775 ---
- 2 -
that an adoption order has been granted under the provisions 
of the   Juvenile    Justice  (Care  and Protection of
Children) Act, 2015.
The submission  of learned senior counsel is placed on 
record.
Consequently, the adoption under HAMA stands revoked.
We direct Central Adoption Resource Authority (CARA) to 
process the application made by the petitioners herein at the
earliest and in accordance with law. 
List after three weeks.
(NEETU SACHDEVA)                                (DIVYA BABBAR)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

1

--- Page 787 ---
                   Mr. Ilin Saraswat, Adv.
                   Ms. Ilma Saifi, Adv.
                   
           UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Heard  learned  counsel  for  the  parties  and
perused the material placed on record.
Admittedly, the petitioners did not approach the
Sessions  Court  for  anticipatory  bail  and  directly
approached the High Court. The High Court dismissed the
application as not maintainable for the said reason.
Although, the petitioners were granted interim
protection from the High Court and also from this
Court, we are not inclined to continue further with
this petition. We, accordingly, dismiss this special
leave petition leaving it open for the petitioners to
approach the Sessions Court for anticipatory bail.
However, considering that the petitioners have been
granted protection during this period, we grant fifteen
days further protection from arrest to the petitioners.
In the meantime, the petitioners may approach
the  Sessions  Court,  which  shall  consider  the
anticipatory  bail  application  on  its  own  merits
uninfluenced by any observations made by the High Court
or on account of the interim protection provided by
this Court.
Pending application(s) shall stand disposed of.
(NEETU KHAJURIA)
ASSISTANT REGISTRAR-CUM-PS
      (RANJANA SHAILEY)
     ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  13-01-2020
in CRA No. 330/2016 passed by the High Court of Kerala at
Ernakulam]
GOPINATHAN PILLAI                                  Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF KERALA                                    Respondent(s)
(IA No. 190942/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN FILING SLP)
 
Date : 14-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.B. PARDIWALA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) : Ms. Asha Gopalan Nair, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Delay condoned.
2. Leave granted
3. The appellant was put to trial in connection with FIR
No.996 of 2014 dated 19.07.2014 under Section 302 of the Indian
Penal Code, 1860 registered at Police Station Puthoor, Kollam. 
4. The appeal filed by the appellant was rejected by the High
Court. The appellant has been in custody for more than eleven
years.
5. We heard Ms. Asha Gopalan Nair, learned Advocate-on-Record
appearing for the appellant.

--- Page 801 ---
2
6. On a prima facie consideration, we are of the opinion that
the conviction of the appellant in SC No. 624/2015 arising out
of the abovementioned FIR affirmed by the High Court based on
circumstantial  evidence  requires  consideration,  especially
looking  at  the  relationship  between  the  accused  and  the
deceased; father and son, coupled with the wayward habits and
conduct of the son, on which suggestions were made, relied on
by the High Court, surprisingly as against the father. 
7. The sentence imposed on the appellant is suspended and he
is ordered to be released on bail, subject to terms and
conditions that the trial court may deem fit and proper to
impose.
8. Notify the main appeal for final hearing in February 2027.
 (VISHAL ANAND)                                    (POOJA SHARMA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                                 COURT MASTER (NSH)

We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner and
learned senior counsel and learned counsel appearing for
the respondents.
We  dispose  of  this  Special  Leave  Petition  by
reserving liberty to the petitioner herein to make a
detailed representation to the to the Vice Chancellor of
the respondent(s)-Dr.Ram Manohar Lohia Avadh University
with regard to the enunciation of a policy with regard to
differently  abled  candidates  who  are  pursuing  their
respective courses under the ageis of the University
having regard to the Rights of Persons with Disabilities
(RPwD) Act, 2016 and in accordance with law.
If such a representation is made by the petitioner
herein, the same shall be considered as expeditiously as
possible and in accordance with law and reply on the said
consideration shall be communicated to the petitioner
herein.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.

HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARUN PALLI
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Mr. Amit Kumar Pathak, AOR
For Respondent(s) :         
                   Mr. Pradeep Misra, AOR
                   Mr. Daleep Dhyani, Adv.
                   Mr. Suraj Singh, Adv.
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr. Amit Kumar Pathak, learned counsel appearing
for the review petitioners submits that review petitioner
No.1 has since deceased.  Learned counsel prays for a
week’s time to file an application for substitution.

                             O R D E R

length.

application(s), if any.

Date : 15-07-2026 These matters were called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIJAY BISHNOI
For Petitioner(s) :  Petitioner-in-person
                    
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Miscellaneous Applications seek direction to the High Court to
dispose of RFA No. 371 of 2018 in a time bound manner.
2. It appears from the record that in RFA No. 371 of 2018, an
interim order was passed which was subjected to a challenge
through Special Leave Petition which came to be dismissed on
14.01.2019.
3. In such circumstances, this Miscellaneous Applications are
misconceived. However, the petitioner may apply to the High Court
for expeditious hearing of the pending appeal. With the aforesaid
liberty, this applications stand disposed of.
4. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (KAVITA PAHUJA)                               (SAPNA BANSAL)

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R

of the incident attributing main role of firing at the deceased
to the petitioner, we decline to interfere with the impugned
order though without expressing any opinion on the merits of
the appeal.
2. The petition is, accordingly, dismissed by giving liberty
to the petitioner to apply for expediting the hearing of the
pending appeal before the High Court.
3.  Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
